title: Amplitude Control (Two-Pulse Coherent Control)
description: Raw BCTDS homodyne I/Q measurements of a two-pulse coherent-control protocol on TLS defect ensembles, measured on three different samples. The amplitude of the first pulse is swept while the inter-pulse spacing is varied, probing amplitude-controlled memory effects between the two pulses. Only the in-phase (I) and quadrature (Q) signals are measured; magnitude, phase, and FFT spectra are computable from them and are not stored.

samples.name: AlOx
samples.material: Sapphire substrate with 2 nm ALD AlOx layer

apparatus.waveguide: WR-229 rectangular waveguide, 3-6 GHz passband, TE10 mode

timing.note: The instrument specifies and records time in integer hardware clock cycles, converted to nanoseconds using the relevant clock rate. Pulse duration is fixed for this experiment; the first pulse's amplitude is the primary swept variable.

constant_drive_parameters.pulse_phase: the phase of both pulses is fixed for this experiment
constant_drive_parameters.amplitude_note: The swept amplitude_arb column is the FIRST pulse's amplitude; the second pulse is held at a fixed ~10000 a.u. reference. That second value is recorded nowhere in the raw files — neither in the filenames nor in the arrays.
